title: 2026 F1 Australian GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: The factory Mercedes team - George Russell heading Kimi Antonelli - duly finished one-two in Melbourne on Sunday, exhibiting more power, more downforce, more grip and more balance than all of their opposition - and capped all of that with perfect strategy calls. Ferrari pushed them hardest, with Charles Leclerc taking the lead into T1 and exchanging early-lap power surges with George Russell, but weird strategies quickly knocked them back to P3-P4, with Charles Leclerc crossing the line 0.6sec a

title: 2026 F1 Australian GP RACE Analysis - Melbourne
description:
2026 F1 Australian GP RACE Analysis in Melbourne! 

What a race in Melbourne. The 2026 season explodes into life at the Australian Grand Prix with a huge result for Mercedes-AMG Petronas Formula One Team.

George Russell delivers a flawless drive to take victory around Albert Park, controlling the race from the front and managing the new-era F1 machinery perfectly. But the real headline might be the incredible performance from rookie sensation Kimi Antonelli, bringing the car home in P2 to secur

title: The 2026 F1 Reset Explained
description:
Formula 1 is heading for one of the biggest rule changes in its history.
In 2026, the sport will introduce completely new power units, revised aerodynamics, and major sustainability goals that could reshape the competitive order.

But what exactly is changing? And which teams could benefit the most?

In this video, we break down F1’s Great 2026 Reset — from the new hybrid engine regulations and increased electrical power to the arrival of new manufacturers and the impact on teams and drivers.

title: 2026 F1 Engine & Battery Deployement Explained (MGU-K)
description:
F1 2026 just introduced one tiny word in the regulations — “pending” — and it completely changes how drivers use the throttle on corner exit.

In this video I break down what a power-limited pending period actually means in driver language, why the car no longer gives a sudden hybrid “kick”, and how the 150kW rule forces drivers to commit to throttle earlier instead of waiting for a boost moment.

You’ll learn:
• What “power-limited” vs “traction-limited” really feels like in the car
• Why the b
